summary: Re-ran backend pytest (11/11 passed in 13.14s, down from ~30s) confirming the parallel+retry scan refactor. Live curl scan completed in 9.6s (well under 20s budget). 502 error wrapper compiles and is correctly raised on partial LLM failure; happy path verified. PDF download endpoints still return application/pdf with %PDF- header. Frontend playwright check: dashboard scan flow navigates to /scan/{id}, both PDF buttons trigger inline file downloads (no new tab opened, tab count stays at 1), button shows '...' state during download, files saved with correct .pdf names and %PDF- headers.
critical_code_review_comments: scan_resume parallel+retry refactor is clean - asyncio.gather(return_exceptions=True) + _retry_llm(2 attempts) + 502 wrapper correctly identify which step failed in the error message.
MAX_RESUME_CHARS=12000 and MAX_JD_CHARS=8000 truncation is sensible for prompt-size and latency control.

ScanResult.downloadPdf uses axios responseType:'blob' + URL.createObjectURL + <a download> + revokeObjectURL(1s) - correct pattern, no leaks. Downloading state ('resume'|'cover'|null) properly disables and shows '...'.
Minor: catch blocks in downloadPdf swallow the error without logging - consider console.error for production debugging.
test_credentials: Reused seeded mongosh session_token=test_session_1737000000 (user_id=test-user-1737000000, name=Jane Doe) from iteration 1. Session still valid through 2026-06-05.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data; existing session reused.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Scan pipeline is now ~10s (was ~30s). Backend tests run in ~13s total. If you re-seed the session, update SESSION_TOKEN in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py line 9. Frontend downloads happen inline via Blob + <a download> - playwright must use page.expect_download() to catch them.
rca of the issue: N/A - all targeted features verified working.
